summary: High‑energy 1966 show highlighted by an inspired Cold Rain & Snow and early Smokestack Lightning, captured on a solid soundboard source with only minor cut‑off issues.
review: Fans rave about the electrifying performance – especially the powerful rendition of Cold Rain & Snow, the early‑era Smokestack Lightning jam and a spirited segue into King Bee. The recording is generally clear (soundboard) with just a brief drop‑out after “Dancin’”, making it an enjoyable listen for Deadheads.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give five stars and praise the musical performance; only one low‑star comment is unrelated to quality. Recording is described as good with minor issues, leading to a strong overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear and balanced throughout; slight cut‑off after “Dancin’” noted by one reviewer but no major distortion or muddiness reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set is described as high‑energy with a strong early momentum; despite some confusion about song order, the overall pacing and transitions (e.g., jam into King Bee) are praised.

band_member_comments.Keys: Pig (Ron "Pigpen" McKernan) is highlighted as shining on this performance.
